questions.question: Find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 4 & -2 \\ 3 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
If $\begin{pmatrix} a & 2 \\ 1 & 3 \end{pmatrix}$ has determinant −5, find the value of $a$.
questions.explanation: Determinant = 3a − 2(1) = 3a − 2. Setting 3a − 2 = −5 gives 3a = −3, so a = −1. Wait, recheck: 3a − 2 = −5 → 3a = −3 → a = −1. Hmm, let me verify: 3(−1) − 2 = −3 − 2 = −5. ✓ Answer: a = −1

questions.question: Multiply $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 & 2 \end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: First row: 1(2) + 2(1) + 0(3) = 2 + 2 + 0 = 4. Second row: 3(2) + 1(1) + 2(3) = 6 + 1 + 6 = 13. Actually: 3(2) + 1(1) + 2(3) = 6 + 1 + 6 = 13. Let me recalculate: Second row: 3(2) + 1(1) + 2(3) = 6 + 1 + 6 = 13. So answer is $\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 13 \end{pmatrix}$

questions.question: Two stores sell notebooks and pens. Store A sells 3 notebooks and 5 pens for \$14. Store B sells 2 notebooks and 7 pens for \$16. Set up a matrix equation for the system and identify the coefficient matrix.
questions.explanation: The system is 3n + 5p = 14 and 2n + 7p = 16. The coefficient matrix has the coefficients of n and p in each equation as columns.

questions.question: For matrices $A = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 \end{pmatrix}$ and $B = \begin{pmatrix} 4 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{pmatrix}$, find the sum $A + B$.
